text.title: graph-rag-indexing Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for graph-rag-indexing as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Re-running indexing on the same corpus without using a versioned graph namespace or progress checkpoint.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Entity extraction LLM calls cost $4-20 per million tokens. A 10k-doc corpus without checkpointing wastes the full cost on every crash or restart. Use a content-hash progress file and a versioned collection prefix (e.g., corpus-v2-2026-04) so a re-index does not corrupt an in-use production graph.

failure-mode.description: Assuming all entities in a corpus are present in the graph.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLM entity extraction has recall of 70-85%. Agents must fall back to vector search for entities not found in the graph rather than reporting "no information found".
